---
title: "appendix.qmd"
format: qmd
editor: visual
---

## AI Ackowledgement

1.  I acknowlege the use of [ChatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/c/69cf2aa2-32c0-8324-8725-8305c3d6baf7)to assist with clarifying statistical concepts, improving code implementation in R, and refining the clarity and academic tone of written explanations. I entered the prompts as following:

-   Explanation of linear regression output, including the interpretation of coefficients, slope, intercept, p-values, and R-squared

-   Clarification of the meaning of slope and how to interpret it in the context of marketing spend and purchase amount

-   Guidance on how to determine whether two variables are correlated

-   Instructions on how to extract values (e.g., p-value, coefficients) using inline R code in Quarto

-   Debugging R code errors, particularly related to `lm()` models and grouped analysis

-   Assistance with extracting regression slopes for different customer segments

-   Clarification on whether slopes can be interpreted directly from plots

-   Help with translating Chinese sentences into clear and academic English

-   Refinement of written explanations for regression results and data descriptions

2.  I acknowledge the use of [ChatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/c/69cdcea1-3b54-8322-b12c-aa18a2c2d4b0) to assist with understanding technical concepts, debugging code, and improving written explanations. I entered the prompts as following:

-   What does "se" mean in ggplot / geom_smooth?

-    Why does my figure reference show a question mark (?) when using \@fig-segment-scatter?

-   I used \@fig-segment-scatter in my text, but the cross-reference is not working. Why?

-   What does the instruction mean: "you must also include a paragraph describing these results and how they link back to your research question"?

-   What is the difference between label and caption in Quarto?

-   Why does Quarto show a question mark when referencing figures created in the same code chunk with multiple labels?

-   How should I structure figures and labels correctly in Quarto for cross-referencing?

3.  I acknowledge the use of [ChatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/c/69cdaa95-e084-8324-bd92-854e5b629ea3) to assist with elped clarify statistical concepts (e.g., median vs mean, p-values, and linear regression interpretation), troubleshoot R and Quarto errors. I entered the prompts as following:

-   How should I structure figures and labels correctly in Quarto for cross-referencing?

<!-- -->

-   What is the difference between median and average?”

<!-- -->

-   “When should I use median marketing spend and when should I use average marketing spend?”

-   “What is the English term for sample size?”

-   “Does a p-value of 1.44e-13 mean the correlation is very weak?”

-   “Explain the difference between median and average.”

<!-- -->

-   How should I structure figures and labels correctly in Quarto for cross-referencing?

<!-- -->

-   What is the difference between median and average?”

<!-- -->

-   “When should I use median marketing spend and when should I use average marketing spend?”

-   “What is the English term for sample size?”

-   “Does a p-value of 1.44e-13 mean the correlation is very weak?”

-   “Explain the difference between median and average.”

<!-- -->

-   “Can I use a linear regression model by segment to analyze whether higher marketing spend per customer is associated with higher purchase amounts?”

-   “What does this mean: lm(purchase_amount \~ marketing_spend, data = subset(data2, segment == 'A'))?”

-   “How should I interpret the regression results?”

-   “Explain the regression output (summary results).”

<!-- -->

-   “Explain this ggplot code: ggplot(data2, aes(x = marketing_spend, y = purchase_amount)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = 'lm', se = FALSE).”

-   “How do I adjust the width in geom_col?”

-   “How do I round numbers to 2 decimal places?”

-   “Why doesn’t summarise() work with round() in this way?”

-   “What does ‘counts of certain variables in groups’ mean?”

4.  I acknowledge the use of [Gemini](https://gemini.google.com/app/a3fcb1a069c3bf4e) to assist with technical implementation of R, statistical questions clarification and academic writing. Please find the prompts as following:

-   How do I use inline code to extract these three slopes separately from the lm summary output?

-   How can I see the slope of a data set after it is divided into different segments for an lm model?

-   The Enterprise segment exhibits the steepest gradient... How can I say this more academically in English?
